title: [FIRRTLToHW] Simplify lowerRegConnect, support HW wires
body:
Simplify the `lowerRegConnect` function such that it rejects subfield, subindex, and subaccesses on its left hand side. Consensus after some discussion in #4826 is that any subaccesses on the left hand side of a connect should be handled in a pre-pass that refactors the assignment in the exact way that `lowerRegConnect` used to do until now. This significantly simplifies the implementation of `LowerToHW` and moves the complexities of sink subaccesses into a dedicated pass.

Rename the `lowerRegConnect` function to `lowerConnect` and make it work with `hw::WireOp` in addition to `seq::FirRegOp`.

This removes the need to carry a separate `regMapping` for the backedges used as the `next` value of registers. Instead, `lowerConnect` directly inspects the operation on the LHS of the connect and does the right thing if it's a `seq::FirRegOp` or `hw::WireOp`.

The lowering of FIRRTL connect and strictconnect now first attempt to materialize connects in wires and regs directly through `lowerConnect`, and only if that's not possible (e.g. because the LHS is not a `seq::FirRegOp` or `hw::WireOp`) does it resort to generating assign ops.
